data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ferretti S.p.A. ( (IT:YACHT) ) has shared an update.

KKCG Maritime has launched a voluntary conditional partial public tender offer to acquire up to 52,132,861 shares of Ferretti S.p.A., equivalent to 15.4% of its share capital, and by 9 April 2026 had received valid acceptances for 16,054,043 shares, or about 4.74% of shares in issue. Together with its pre-existing 14.5% holding, KKCG Maritime’s accepted tenders signal a bid to strengthen its strategic position in Ferretti, while further announcements on acceptance levels and final allocation will clarify the ultimate size of its stake and potential influence over the yacht maker’s governance and future direction.

More about Ferretti S.p.A.

Ferretti S.p.A. is a yacht manufacturer listed in Hong Kong, operating in the luxury leisure marine industry with a focus on high-end yachts. Its shares trade under stock code 09638.HK and it attracts strategic investors interested in expanding stakes in the premium boating and maritime lifestyle segment.

data.title: Infinity Logistics Swings to 2025 Loss on Higher Provisions and FX Hit
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited ( (HK:1442) ) has issued an update.

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited reported audited consolidated results for the year ended 31 December 2025, showing a slight decline in revenue to RM452.7 million from RM475.8 million and a modest drop in gross profit, reflecting a softer operating environment in its logistics and transport business. The group’s performance was further pressured by a sharp increase in loss allowances for receivables and higher administrative expenses, which, together with finance costs and tax charges, swung the company from a prior-year profit to a net loss attributable to shareholders, signaling near-term challenges for profitability and potentially heightened risk awareness among investors and other stakeholders.

Total comprehensive income also turned negative, weighed down by foreign exchange translation losses, underscoring the group’s exposure to currency movements across its operating markets. Basic and diluted earnings per share moved from positive territory to a small loss, highlighting the deterioration in shareholder returns and suggesting that management may face greater scrutiny over capital allocation, credit control, and cost management as the company seeks to stabilize results and protect its market position in the logistics sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1442) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited stock, see the HK:1442 Stock Forecast page.

More about Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited

Infinity Logistics and Transport Ventures Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the logistics and transport sector. The group generates revenue primarily from logistics services and related goods, focusing on integrated transport solutions and supply chain support for its customers in regional and international markets.

Manulife Financial ( (TSE:MFC) ) has provided an update.

Manulife Financial Corporation has scheduled its 2026 annual meeting of common shareholders, alongside the annual meeting of policyholders and the shareholder of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company, for May 14, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. Eastern time in Toronto with a concurrent live webcast. The hybrid format enables shareholders, policyholders, and duly appointed proxyholders to ask questions and vote electronically or in person, underscoring the company’s continued emphasis on accessibility and engagement in its governance process.

Investors and policyholders are being strongly encouraged to vote and submit proxies ahead of the meeting date, with detailed instructions and materials available on Manulife’s annual meeting website. Policyholders of The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company are also provided with a formal channel to request notice of future meetings, reinforcing transparency and ongoing communication between the insurer and its stakeholders.

More about Manulife Financial

Manulife Financial Corporation is a global financial services group that provides insurance, wealth management, and asset management solutions to individual and institutional clients. Through its primary insurance subsidiary, The Manufacturers Life Insurance Company, Manulife focuses on life insurance, retirement, and investment products across key markets including Canada and internationally.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mexan ( (HK:0022) ) has provided an update.

Mexan Limited has announced the completion of a major share placing by its controlling shareholder, referred to as the Vendor, involving 1,326,735,354 existing shares, or about 67.47% of the company’s issued share capital. These shares were placed by a placing agent to at least six independent investors, each restricted from holding 30% or more of the company after the transaction, in line with Hong Kong takeover regulations.

Following completion of the placing on 9 April 2026, the Vendor ceased to hold any shares and is no longer the controlling shareholder of Mexan, marking a significant change in the company’s ownership structure. Two of the placees, investor Chen Jixiang and Max Premier Limited, which he ultimately owns, together acquired around 17.64% of Mexan’s shares and have become substantial shareholders, prompting the company to advise existing shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its stock.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0022) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.25 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Mexan stock, see the HK:0022 Stock Forecast page.

More about Mexan

Mexan Limited, listed in Hong Kong under stock code 22, operates as a publicly traded company with a diverse shareholder base and a board comprising executive and independent non-executive directors. The firm is overseen by chairman Lun Yiu Kay Edwin, with corporate governance structured in line with Hong Kong listing rules and market regulations.

data.title: Sino Biopharm Advances Kylo-11 With First U.S. Patient in Phase II Trial
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sino Biopharmaceutical ( (HK:1177) ) has provided an update.

Sino Biopharmaceutical announced that its subsidiary Hangzhou Hygieia Biomedical has enrolled the first U.S. patient in a Phase II clinical trial of Kylo-11, an siRNA drug targeting the LPA gene for patients with atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and elevated lipoprotein(a). The study, conducted in both China and the United States, will test dosing regimens of once every six months or once a year, positioning the therapy as a potential best-in-class, ultra-long-acting treatment for a major, currently unaddressed cardiovascular risk factor.

Kylo-11 uses a dual-conjugated siRNA delivery platform that has received global patent authorization and aims to deliver durable reductions in Lp(a) levels with favorable safety and tolerability. Early Phase I data showed robust and sustained lowering of Lp(a) across multiple dose cohorts with mostly mild, unrelated adverse events, underscoring the drug’s potential to fill a global therapeutic gap and strengthen Sino Biopharmaceutical’s presence in innovative cardiovascular therapies.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1177) stock is a Buy with a HK$8.70 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sino Biopharmaceutical stock, see the HK:1177 Stock Forecast page.

More about Sino Biopharmaceutical

Sino Biopharmaceutical Limited is a China-based pharmaceutical group engaged in the research, development, manufacturing, and commercialization of innovative medicines. Through its wholly owned subsidiary Hangzhou Hygieia Biomedical, the company focuses on cutting-edge RNA interference therapies, targeting major unmet needs in cardiovascular and other chronic diseases in global markets.

Average Trading Volume: 70,575,576

An announcement from Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9881) ) is now available.

Rongta Technology (Xiamen) Group Co., Ltd. has delayed publication of its audited annual results for the year ended 31 December 2025, as its audit committee and external auditors continue to review certain transactions, including related-party guaranteed bank loans, overseas advertising prepayments, fund investments via licensed corporations, and convertible bond subscriptions. An independent investigation led by an external law firm has found no evidence of material financial risk, lack of commercial substance, or fraud in these matters, but trading in the company’s shares will remain suspended until the results are finalised, with management aiming to release the figures within April 2026 and urging investors to exercise caution.

The latest update is out from Tian Ge Interactive Holdings ( (HK:1980) ).

Tian Ge Interactive Holdings has disclosed that its current public float stands at about 20.85%, below the minimum level required under Hong Kong Listing Rules, largely due to ongoing share offer activities by major shareholder-affiliated parties. The company notes that a pre-conditional voluntary cash partial offer by Sina for 32,500,000 shares remains open after being extended, and full public shareholder acceptance would reduce the public float further to roughly 17.92%.

Because the partial offer period is still in progress, the board is restricted by takeover regulations from taking remedial actions to increase the free float until the offer closes. Management has reiterated its commitment to restoring compliance with the minimum public float requirement as soon as practicable after the offer period ends and will update investors on both its waiver application to the exchange and any steps taken to rebuild the public float.

An update from ALSCO Pooling Service Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2649) ) is now available.

ALSCO Pooling Service Co., Ltd., a Hong Kong-listed joint stock company incorporated in the PRC, has announced corporate governance measures in response to audit concerns over a significant transaction. The board includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, and the company is subject to Hong Kong listing and securities regulations governing disclosure and inside information.

The company has formed a special committee of its independent non-executive directors to investigate an approximately RMB73 million transfer made in late February 2026 and its compliance with listing rules. An external independent accounting firm has been appointed as forensic investigator, and trading in the company’s H shares will remain suspended while the probe continues, prompting the company to urge shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution.

The special committee, chaired by independent director Hong Ting, will examine the background and impact of the transaction and report findings and recommendations to the board. Jiashili Group Limited has restructured key governance roles, with chief financial officer Liu Yun stepping down as company secretary and authorised representative as part of an internal reorganisation, while retaining her finance role. Wenzhou Kangning Hospital Co ( (HK:2120) ) has issued an announcement.

Wenzhou Kangning Hospital has disclosed that its public float currently stands at about 24.11%, below Hong Kong listing rules that require at least 25% of its issued shares to be held by the public. The latest announcement is out from Greentown China Holdings ( (HK:3900) ).

Greentown China Holdings reported unaudited operating data for the first quarter of 2026, highlighting sales momentum in both self-investment and project management businesses. The group sold 2,840 units in March with a sales area of about 420,000 square meters and monthly sales of roughly RMB15.1 billion at an average price above RMB36,000 per square meter.

For the first three months of 2026, Greentown recorded contracted sales area of 820,000 square meters totaling about RMB25.6 billion, of which RMB17.7 billion was attributable to the group, alongside additional subscription sales of RMB3.1 billion. Its project management segment contributed sales of roughly RMB13.0 billion on 940,000 square meters, bringing total group sales area to about 1.76 million square meters and total contracted sales to approximately RMB38.6 billion for the quarter.

The figures underscore Greentown’s substantial scale and ongoing demand for its projects despite being preliminary and unaudited, suggesting resilient cash flow generation and continued relevance in China’s challenging property market. Bright Smart Securities & Commodities Group Limited has announced a broad reshuffle of its senior leadership, effective 9 April 2026, including the appointment of non-executive director Huang Hao as chairman, replacing executive director Yip Mow Lum. The move signals a shift in board leadership from an executive to a non-executive chair, which may influence the company’s governance dynamics and strategic oversight.

The group also named executive director Zheng Yanlan as chief executive officer, with former CEO and executive director Hui Yik Bun redesignated as co-chief executive officer to handle specific operations as determined by the board. Zheng has signed a new three-year service agreement with a defined remuneration package and potential discretionary bonus, underscoring the board’s effort to formalize and incentivize the new management structure while maintaining continuity through Hui’s ongoing operational role.

In addition to these appointments, the company announced concurrent changes to its authorised representative, company secretary, and the composition of its board committees. These comprehensive governance adjustments indicate a coordinated leadership transition that could affect decision-making processes and regulatory liaison, with implications for how the group manages compliance, corporate strategy, and stakeholder engagement going forward.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1428) stock is a Hold with a HK$12.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bright Smart Securities & Commodities Group Limited stock, see the HK:1428 Stock Forecast page.

More about Bright Smart Securities & Commodities Group Limited

Bright Smart Securities & Commodities Group Limited is a Hong Kong-listed financial services group incorporated in the Cayman Islands. The company operates in the securities and commodities sector, providing brokerage and related investment services to clients in the Hong Kong market and beyond.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hainan Drinda New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2865) ) has shared an update.

Hainan Drinda New Energy Technology reported first-quarter 2026 operating revenue of RMB 1.69 billion, down 9.63% year on year, but swung to a net profit attributable to shareholders of RMB 14.16 million from a significant loss a year earlier. Net profit after deducting non-recurring items remained negative at RMB -43.50 million, while total assets were broadly stable at RMB 16.28 billion and shareholder equity rose about 10%, suggesting some balance sheet strengthening despite weaker sales.

The company’s basic and diluted earnings per share improved to RMB 0.05, and weighted average return on net assets turned slightly positive at 0.37%, reflecting improved profitability metrics versus last year’s deep losses. However, net cash flow from operating activities deteriorated sharply to a negative RMB 703.64 million, highlighting ongoing cash pressure and execution risk even as headline profitability recovers, which will be closely watched by investors and creditors.

data.title: Forgame Takes Delivery of Longfor Shares After Equity-Linked Note Triggers Disclosable Deal
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Forgame Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:0484) ) is now available.

Forgame Holdings Limited has disclosed that it entered into an equity-linked fixed coupon note in September 2025 with a principal amount of US$750,000, linked to shares of Longfor Group Holdings, Vistra Corp and NetEase Inc. The note, issued by BNP Paribas through its Hong Kong branch and funded entirely by Forgame’s internal cash, carried a six-month tenor and an 18.37% coupon.

Following the final valuation on 2 April 2026, the underlying share price of Longfor fell below the strike price, obliging Forgame to take delivery of 736,559 Longfor shares at approximately US$1.023 per share, for a total of about US$750,000 excluding transaction costs. As one of the applicable percentage ratios for this acquisition exceeds 5% but remains below 25%, the transaction is classified as disclosable under Hong Kong listing rules, underscoring Forgame’s active use of structured products in its treasury and investment operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0484) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.86 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Forgame Holdings Ltd. stock, see the HK:0484 Stock Forecast page.

Forgame Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 484. The group is engaged in investment activities that include trading and holding of listed securities as part of its broader capital management strategy.
